function.name: web_search
function.description: Search the public internet for information. Each result will contain a url, a title, and one excerpt taken directly from the page.

start_date.description: (optional) The start date for the search range in the format YYYY-MM-DD. Must not be equal to end_date.

end_date.description: (optional) The end date for the search range in the format YYYY-MM-DD. If the value is later than 2026-03-01, it will be set to 2026-03-01.

number_of_results.description: (optional) The number of search results to return.

function.name: retrieve_information
function.description: Retrieves critical data segments from a previously ingested document via hybrid lexical and semantic calculations. Crucial Requirement: The search prompt argument MUST contain the token placeholder containing your storage key inside double curly brackets, exactly format formatted as: {{key_name}} alongside your query description text.

prompt.description: Your target question containing the explicit target reference mapping (e.g., 'What were the total research costs in {{my_document_key}}?').

preserve_document_order.description: Set to True if processing complex structural data dependencies across sequential disclosures.

function.name: parse_html_page
function.description: Parses an HTML document or SEC filing. It automatically extracts key metadata, converts asset tables to clean markdown, slices sections into optimal word chunks, and runs concurrent contextual LLM enrichment before updating the disk cache.

url.description: The target URL of the HTML page/filing to process.

key.description: The unique cache storage key for saving and loading the index.

function.name: edgar_search
function.description: Search the EDGAR Database through the SEC API. You should provide a search query. You can also optionally provide a start date, an end date, a page number, top N results, a list of form types, and/or a list of CIKs. The results are returned as a list of dictionaries, each containing the metadata for a filing. It does not contain the full text of the filing.

search_query.description: The case-insensitive search-term or phrase to search the contents of filings and their attachments. This can be a single word, phrase, or combination of words and phrases. Supported search features include wildcards (*), Boolean operators (OR, NOT), and exact phrase matching by enclosing phrases in quotation marks ("exact phrase"). By default, all terms are joined by an implicit AND operator.

form_types.description: (optional) Limits search to specific EDGAR form types (e.g., ['8-K', '10-Q']) list of strings. Default: all form types

ciks.description: (optional) Filters results to filings from specified CIKs, type list of strings. Leading zeros are optional but may be included. Example: [ "0001811414", "1318605" ]. Default: all CIKs

start_date.description: (optional) Start date for the search range in yyyy-mm-dd format. If the value is a date that is later than 2026-03-01, it will be set to 2026-03-01.

end_date.description: (optional) End date for the search range, in the same format as startDate. If the value is a date that is later than 2026-03-01, it will be set to 2026-03-01.

page.description: (optional) Used for pagination. Each page contains up to 100 matching filings. Increase the page number to retrieve the next set of 100 filings. Example: 3 retrieves the third page. Default: 1

top_n_results.description: (optional) Return only the first N results out of 100 from the page. If not provided, all 100 results will be returned. E.g. if page is 2, and top_n_results is 10, you will receive results 100 to 110.

function.name: calculator
function.description: Evaluate a mathematical expression and return the result. Use this tool for all arithmetic calculations instead of computing by hand. Supports: +, -, *, /, ** (exponentiation), % (modulo), and parentheses for grouping. Available functions: abs(), min(), max(), sqrt(), log(), log10(). Examples: '(5000 - 3200) * 0.21', '(2865507 / 1905871) ** 0.5 - 1', '14060 / 2148'.

function.name: price_history
function.description: Fetch historical daily price data for a specific asset class. Returns a CSV table with one row per day. Use asset_class='equity' or 'etf' for US-listed stocks/ETFs (e.g. AAPL, SPY), 'crypto' for pairs like btcusd (lowercase, no dash), or 'fx' for pairs like audusd. Non-US equities and most indices/futures are not covered by the pricing provider. Each row includes raw OHLC (open/high/low/close), split- and dividend-adjusted OHLC (adjOpen/adjHigh/adjLow/adjClose), raw and adjusted volume, divCash (dividend amount on the date), and splitFactor.

ticker.description: Ticker symbol. High-level symbology rules: (1) Equity/ETF/mutual fund: use the US exchange-listed symbol (AAPL, SPY, VFIAX). Symbols are case-insensitive and contain only letters and dashes. (2) Share classes use a dash, not a dot: 'BRK-A' / 'BRK-B' (not 'BRK.A'), 'BF-B'. (3) Securities are identified by the ticker as listed on the primary US exchange; non-US listings, OTC pink sheets, indices, and futures are generally not covered. (4) Tickers can be reused after delisting; data is point-in-time for the entity that held the ticker on each date. (5) Crypto: concatenated lowercase pair with no separator, e.g. 'btcusd', 'ethusd'. (6) FX: concatenated lowercase pair with no separator, e.g. 'audusd', 'eurusd'.

asset_class.description: Asset class used to route to the proper pricing endpoint.

function.name: submit_final_result
function.description: Submits the final answer to the user. You should include your final answer, as well as any necessary reasoning, justification, calculations, and explanation. Finally, you should provide any sources used to answer the question. You MUST use this tool to submit your final result. The user will not see your response if you do not use this tool to submit. You will not be able to continue working after this tool is called; the conversation will be ended.

final_result.description: The final result to submit to the user

steps.message:
You are an IPO and financial diligence agent.

You are given a question and must answer it using the tools provided.

You cannot interact with the user or ask clarifying questions.

Answer solely based on available information and tools.

Assume the current date is May 31, 2026.

---

TOOLS AND DATA SOURCES

You have access to a document storage system.

You may store parsed HTML retrieved from the web and later retrieve
information using LLM-based retrieval prompts.

Use this system to avoid context-window limitations.

When working with long filings, prefer section-specific retrieval prompts
rather than broad document-level retrieval.

Examples:

- retrieve the Segment Adjusted EBITDA reconciliation table from MD&A
- retrieve the glossary definition of Starshield
- retrieve the controlled-company governance disclosure

For peer-comparison questions:

- retrieve peer SEC filings using edgar_search
- do not rely on memory for peer financial figures

For historical public-equity price data not available in SEC filings:

- use price_history as primary source
- use raw unadjusted prices unless adjusted prices are requested
- fall back to web_search only if price_history fails

Stock indices are not covered by price_history.

Use authoritative sources such as FRED or web_search for index levels.

---

SOURCE HIERARCHY

Prioritize sources in this order:

1. S-1 / S-1A filings
2. Incorporated SEC filings
3. SEC exhibits and merger documents
4. SEC periodic filings
5. Company earnings materials
6. External sources

If figures differ across sources:

prefer the most authoritative SEC source containing the directly reported
figure and explain material discrepancies when relevant.

SEC filings are the primary and most authoritative source of financial and
disclosure information.

---

ANSWER STANDARDS

You must call submit_final_result with the completed answer.

Your submission will not be processed unless submit_final_result is called.

Include:

- complete reasoning
- calculations
- supporting analysis

You will be evaluated on:

- final-answer accuracy
- analytical correctness

Do not round intermediate calculations.

Round only final reported values.

When possible, report calculated figures to at least two decimal places.

Report financial figures using the same scale and units used in the filing
unless otherwise specified.

Share prices should be reported with two decimal places.

---

DISCLOSURE DISCIPLINE

Before reaching a conclusion:

determine whether the filing provides sufficient information to answer:

- directly
- partially
- only through inference

Distinguish carefully among:

- facts explicitly disclosed
- reasonable filing-supported inferences
- information not disclosed

Do not speculate beyond what the filing supports.

If material information is absent:

explicitly state that the filing does not provide sufficient information to
conclude.

Absence of disclosure may itself be analytically material.

---

FORWARD-LOOKING STATEMENTS

Treat separately:

- historical facts
- roadmap targets
- commercialization expectations
- management intentions
- forward-looking statements

Do not present aspirations or plans as historical outcomes.

---

GOVERNANCE AND LEGAL QUESTIONS

For governance, legal, or disclosure-language questions:

use the filing's precise terminology whenever possible.

Distinguish among:

- requirements
- exemptions
- company elections
- forward-looking language

---

ACCOUNTING POLICY QUESTIONS

For accounting-policy questions:

1. Identify the relevant disclosed accounting policy or treatment
2. Determine whether the filing explicitly resolves the issue
3. If unresolved, state that the filing does not specify treatment

---

NON-GAAP QUESTIONS

For non-GAAP measures:

identify:

- the starting GAAP metric
- each disclosed adjustment
- whether economically recurring costs are excluded

Do not accept non-GAAP measures without analyzing their construction.

---

AUDIENCE TAGS

Questions may include audience tags such as:

[PUBLIC | BANK]
[VC | CREDIT]
[LEGAL | ACCOUNTING]

Use these tags to calibrate terminology and analytical emphasis while
remaining grounded in disclosed facts.

---

SOURCES

At the end of your answer provide:

{
  "sources": [
    {
      "url": "...",
      "name": "..."
    }
  ]
}


---


FILING CONTEXT

The primary filing for this benchmark is the SpaceX S-1 filed May 20, 2026:

https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1181412/000162828026036936/spaceexplorationtechnologi.htm

Important:

The consolidated financial statements have been retrospectively recast under
common-control accounting to include:

- xAI (merged February 2, 2026)
- X Holdings (merged into xAI March 28, 2025)

for all historical periods presented.

No period reflects standalone SpaceX operations exclusively.

When interpreting historical segment figures, state whether they reflect the
recast consolidated entity.

SpaceX (SPCX) has no public trading history as of May 30, 2026.

Do not attempt price_history retrieval for SpaceX or SPCX.

For peer valuation or peer financial benchmarking, retrieve peer-company SEC
filings using edgar_search rather than relying on memory.

Question:
The preliminary filing omits IPO price range and final share count. Which of the following can be determined from the filing as filed and which require amendment disclosure: (i) post-IPO fully diluted share count, (ii) Class A versus Class B economic ownership, and (iii) Musk’s post-IPO voting control?

steps.reasoning_content:
The question asks about what can be determined from the SpaceX S-1 filing as filed (filed May 20, 2026) versus what requires amendment disclosure, regarding three specific items:

1. Post-IPO fully diluted share count
2. Class A versus Class B economic ownership
3. Musk's post-IPO voting control

Let me first parse the S-1 filing to understand its contents, then determine what information is available.

Let me start by parsing the filing.

results.content: SUCCESS: Loaded portable assets for key: spacex_s1

steps.reasoning_content: Let me retrieve information about the share structure, IPO details, and voting control from the filing.

arguments.prompt: What is the total number of authorized shares, outstanding shares, and any details about the IPO offering size, share classes, and post-IPO capitalization in {{spacex_s1}}?
What are the details about Class A and Class B common stock, their voting rights, economic rights, and how many shares of each class are outstanding in {{spacex_s1}}?
What is Elon Musk's ownership percentage, voting control, and share holdings in {{spacex_s1}}?

results.content:
Based on the provided S-1 prospectus excerpts, here are the details of the authorized shares, outstanding shares, IPO offering size, share classes, and post-IPO capitalization.

---

### Authorized Shares (Post-IPO)

Upon completion of the offering, the amended charter will authorize:

- **Class A common stock**: 36,132,150,000 shares (par value $0.001)
- **Class B common stock**: 6,125,000,000 shares (par value $0.001)
- **Class C common stock**: 10,000,000,000 shares (par value $0.001) – will be authorized but have **no shares outstanding**
- **Class D common stock**: **Eliminated** (0 shares authorized post-IPO)
- **Preferred stock**: 2,400,000,000 shares (par value $0.001) – authorized but **no shares outstanding** post-IPO

**Total authorized capital stock**: 54,657,150,000 shares  
(Common: 52,257,150,000 + Preferred: 2,400,000,000)

---

### Outstanding Shares

The exact number of Class A shares offered and the final post-IPO outstanding counts are redacted (blank) in the prospectus. However, the pre-IPO outstanding shares after giving effect to the Class C reclassification, preferred stock conversion, and the 2026 5-for-1 stock split are disclosed:

- **Class A common stock**: 6,932,508,000 shares (as of May 1, 2026)
- **Class B common stock**: 5,602,790,410 shares (as of May 1, 2026) — a slightly different pro forma figure of 5,695,729,430 also appears in the capitalization table
- **Class C common stock**: 0 shares (all converted into Class A)
- **Preferred stock**: 0 shares (all converted into Class A and Class B)

After the IPO, the Class A outstanding will increase by the number of shares sold to the public (the “offering size”), while Class B will remain unchanged. The issuer states the final numbers will depend on the IPO price and underwriter option exercise.

---

### IPO Offering Size and Structure

- The offering is for **shares of Class A common stock** only.
- The number of shares offered and the price range are **not disclosed** in the provided text (all figures appear as blanks or “$    ”).
- The underwriters have an option to purchase additional Class A shares (amount not specified).
- The shares are expected to be listed on **Nasdaq** and **Nasdaq Texas** under the symbol **“SPCX.”**
- The offering price is assumed at the **midpoint of the estimated range**, which is also blank.

---

### Share Classes and Voting Structure

- **Class A common stock**: One vote per share.
- **Class B common stock**: Ten votes per share. Class B shareholders, voting as a separate class, elect **51% of the board** (a majority). The remaining directors are elected by Class A and Class B voting together.
- **Class C common stock**: Authorized but no shares will be outstanding post-IPO. (Pre-IPO Class C shares were reclassified into Class A.)
- **Class D common stock**: Eliminated post-IPO (no shares authorized, issued, or outstanding).
- **Preferred stock**: Authorized but wholly undesignated and with no shares outstanding post-IPO.

Elon Musk, through his ownership of Class B shares, will control a substantial majority of the voting power immediately after the offering (exact percentage is redacted), making the company a “controlled company” under Nasdaq rules.

---

### Post-IPO Capitalization (Pro Forma As Adjusted)

The prospectus includes a capitalization table reflecting the offering and related structural changes. The key pro forma as adjusted figures (as of March 31, 2026) are:

| | Pro Forma As Adjusted
|--------------|---------------------
| **Class A common stock** | Shares outstanding (including IPO) — blank
| **Class B common stock** | 5,695,729,430 shares outstanding
| **Class C common stock** | 0 shares outstanding
| **Class D common stock** | 0 shares authorized / outstanding
| **Preferred stock** | 2,400,000,000 authorized; 0 outstanding
| **Additional paid-in capital** | $81,126 million
| **Accumulated deficit** | ($41,311 million)
| **Accumulated other comprehensive income** | $1,755 million
| **Total shareholders’ equity** | **$41,582 million**
| **Total capitalization** | **$70,693 million**

(All dollar amounts are in millions; blanks are due to redacted IPO pricing and share counts. Total capitalization includes debt instruments not detailed in these excerpts.)

---

**Summary**: The post-IPO capital structure will have a dual-class voting system with Class A (one vote) and Class B (ten votes). Authorized shares total ~54.66 billion, with ~12.5 billion shares outstanding before the IPO (Class A ~6.9B, Class B ~5.6B). The exact IPO size and final outstanding counts remain redacted, but the company will have no outstanding Class C, Class D, or preferred stock after the offering.

Based on the provided chunks of the prospectus, here are the details about the Class A and Class B common stock:

### **Voting Rights**

*   **Class A Common Stock:** Entitled to **one vote per share**.
*   **Class B Common Stock:** Entitled to **ten votes per share**.
*   **General Voting:** Holders of Class A and Class B common stock will generally vote together as a single class on all matters submitted to shareholders.
*   **Special Class B Voting Rights (Voting Separately):**
    *   **Board Control:** Holders of Class B common stock, voting separately as a class, are entitled to elect **51% of the total number of authorized directors** (rounded up to the nearest whole number).
    *   **CEO/Chairman Removal:** The removal of Elon Musk from his board and leadership roles (Chief Executive Officer and Chairman) requires the approval of the holders of at least a majority of the voting power of the outstanding Class B shares, voting separately as a class.
    *   **Protective Provisions:** Approval from Class B holders, voting separately as a class, is required for any amendment to the charter that changes the rights, powers, preferences, or privileges of the Class B stock, and for certain combinations.

### **Economic Rights**

The provided text does not explicitly distinguish the economic rights (like dividends or liquidation) between Class A and Class B common stock, implying they are equal:

*   **Dividends:** When declared by the board, dividends will be paid to holders of Class A and Class B common stock **pro rata, on an equal priority, pari passu basis**.
*   **Liquidation:** Upon a liquidation, dissolution, or winding up, holders of Class A and Class B common stock are entitled to share **ratably** in all assets remaining after payment of liabilities and any liquidation preference of preferred stock.
*   **Conversion:** This is a key economic right specific to Class B shares. Each share of Class B common stock is convertible at any time at the option of the holder into **one share of Class A common stock**. It will also automatically convert into one share of Class A upon a transfer, with exceptions for certain "Permitted Transfers."

### **Number of Shares Outstanding**

The documents provide share counts from different periods (pre-IPO current structure vs. post-IPO authorized amounts) and the specific post-IPO outstanding numbers are left blank as they depend on the final offering.

*   **Post-IPO Authorized Shares:**
    *   **Class A:** 36,132,150,000 shares are authorized.
    *   **Class B:** 6,125,000,000 shares are authorized.

*   **Pre-IPO Shares Outstanding (from the Capitalization Table):**
    *   **Class A:** 2,882,444,444 shares outstanding (actual).
    *   **Class B:** 2,421,276,530 shares issued and outstanding (actual).

*   **Pre-IPO Shares Outstanding (from the Balance Sheet):**
    *   **Class A:** 1,954 and 1,832 shares outstanding as of December 31, 2025 and 2024, respectively (Note: these figures appear to be in millions or thousands, contrasting with the detailed numbers in the capitalization table which are likely the full share count).
    *   **Class B:** 644 and 768 shares issued and outstanding as of December 31, 2025 and 2024, respectively.

Based on the beneficial ownership table provided (pre-offering), Elon Musk’s share holdings and voting control break down as follows:

### Pre-Offering (Before This Offering)
| Metric | Amount / Percentage |
|--------|---------------------|
| **Class A common stock** | 849,494,440 shares (12.3% of Class A) |
| **Class B common stock** | 5,569,053,075 shares (93.6% of Class B) |
| **Combined voting power** | **85.1%** |

The combined voting power of 85.1% is calculated because Class B shares carry 10 votes per share while Class A shares carry 1 vote per share, and Musk holds the vast majority of the high-vote Class B stock.

### What the Shares Include (from Footnote 1 to the table)
The 5,569,053,075 Class B shares and 849,494,440 Class A shares are held across multiple trusts and include options, specifically:
- 1,302,072,285 shares of **restricted Class B common stock** (unvested, subject to performance/conditions)
- 842,091,670 Class A and 3,788,654,145 Class B shares held by the **Elon Musk Revocable Trust** (Musk as trustee)
- 900,495 Class B shares held by the **Musk 2017 Sprinkling Trust** (Musk as trustee)
- 7,402,770 Class A shares held by the **EM 2024 GRAT-A** (Musk as trustee)
- 127,426,150 Class B shares held by the **Mission Trust** (Musk as trustee)
- 350,000,000 Class B shares issuable upon exercise of **options** exercisable within 60 days of May 1, 2026
- 237,530 Class A shares are **pledged as security** for personal indebtedness

### Post-Offering Voting Control & “Controlled Company” Status
The post-offering columns in the table are intentionally **left blank** in the prospectus (the final numbers depend on the IPO price and number of shares sold). The narrative sections throughout the document make clear that **immediately after the offering, Elon Musk will retain majority voting control**. Key points:
- He will hold **more than 50% of the combined voting power** of common stock (Class A + Class B voting together).
- As the holder of a majority of the outstanding **Class B common stock**, he will be entitled to **elect 51% of the total board** (the “Class B Directors”) – and for as long as he holds a majority of voting power, he effectively controls the election of **all** directors.
- He can only be removed from his positions as CEO, CTO, and Chairman by a majority vote of the Class B shares (which he controls).
- The company will qualify as a **“controlled company”** under Nasdaq rules, exempting it from certain independence requirements.

Because the exact post-offering percentages are not disclosed, the only concrete numbers available are the **pre-offering holdings of 849.5M Class A shares, 5.57B Class B shares, and 85.1% voting power**. If you need the final post-offering figures, they would appear in a later amended prospectus once the offering price is set.

steps.reasoning_content:
Let me analyze what I've found so far and then dig deeper into each of the three questions.

**Question (i): Post-IPO fully diluted share count**

From the filing:
- The number of Class A shares to be offered is blank/redacted
- The IPO price range is blank/redacted
- The pre-IPO outstanding shares are disclosed: Class A ~6.93B, Class B ~5.60B (after giving effect to the Class C reclassification, preferred stock conversion, and the 2026 5-for-1 stock split)
- Post-IPO Class A outstanding is left blank because it depends on the number of shares offered
- Dilutive securities (options, RSUs, etc.) may also affect fully diluted count

So the filing does NOT disclose the IPO price range or the number of shares to be offered. Without knowing the number of shares to be offered, the post-IPO fully diluted share count cannot be determined. Also, fully diluted would need to account for outstanding options, warrants, etc.

Let me check if there are redemption/exchange details for X Holdings and xAI that would affect fully diluted counts.

Let me also look more carefully at the capitalization table and the beneficial ownership table. I also need to check if there are any earnout shares, additional shares to be issued in connection with the mergers, etc.

Let me do more targeted retrievals.

arguments.prompt: What is the full capitalization table, including pro forma and pro forma as adjusted columns, with all share counts for Class A, Class B, and any dilutive securities like options, RSUs, warrants in {{spacex_s1}}?
Are there any earnout shares, contingent consideration, merger consideration shares, or additional shares to be issued after the IPO that would affect fully diluted share count in {{spacex_s1}}?
What is the total number of shares to be offered in the IPO, the price range, and any details about greenshoe or underwriter option in {{spacex_s1}}?

results.content:
Below is the **Capitalization** table from the SpaceX (Space Exploration Technologies Corp.) S-1 filing as of March 31, 2026, presented in Actual, Pro Forma, and Pro Forma As Adjusted columns. The table includes all share counts for Class A, Class B, Class C, and Class D common stock, as well as preferred stock, in the same format shown in the prospectus.  

Dilutive securities (stock options, RSUs, warrants) are **not** included *in* the capitalization table itself; they are disclosed separately in the prospectus (e.g., in the “Beneficial Ownership” and “Description of Capital Stock” sections). The share reserves for those instruments appear in the footnotes and dilution discussion. I have added a note summarizing the outstanding dilutive securities as disclosed elsewhere in the filing.

---

### Capitalization Table
**(Dollars in millions, except par values and share counts)**

| | **As of March 31, 2026** | | | |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| | **Actual** | **Pro Forma** | **Pro Forma As Adjusted** |
| **Cash and cash equivalents** | $15,852 | $15,852 | $ — |
| **Long-term debt:** | | | |
| SpaceX Credit Facility (1) | $ — | $ — | |
| SpaceX Bridge Loan (2) | 20,000 | 20,000 | |
| X 2027 and X 2030 Notes | 27 | 27 | |
| Other Financings (3) | 9,105 | 9,105 | |
| Unamortized deferred financing costs | (21) | (21) | |
| **Total long-term debt** | $29,111 | $29,111 | |
| | | | |
| **Redeemable convertible preferred stock:** | | | |
| Redeemable convertible preferred stock, par value $0.001; 189,155,861 shares issued and 134,451,267 shares outstanding, actual; no shares authorized, issued or outstanding, pro forma and pro forma as adjusted | $7,049 | $ — | $ — |
| | | | |
| **Shareholders’ equity:** | | | |
| **Class A common stock**, par value $0.001; 2,964,501,353 shares issued and 2,882,444,444 shares outstanding, actual; 36,132,150,000 shares authorized, 6,824,581,339 shares issued and outstanding, pro forma; 36,132,150,000 shares authorized, shares issued and outstanding, pro forma as adjusted | 3 | 6 | — |
| **Class B common stock**, par value $0.001; 2,421,276,530 shares issued and outstanding, actual; 6,125,000,000 shares authorized, 5,695,729,430 shares issued and outstanding, pro forma and pro forma as adjusted | 3 | 6 | — |
| **Class C common stock**, par value $0.001; 494,026,445 shares issued and outstanding, actual; 10,000,000,000 shares authorized, no shares issued or outstanding, pro forma and pro forma as adjusted | 0 | — | — |
| **Class D common stock**, par value $0.0001; no shares issued and outstanding, actual; no shares authorized, issued or outstanding, pro forma and pro forma as adjusted | — | — | — |
| **Preferred stock**, par value $0.001; no shares issued and outstanding, actual; 2,400,000,000 shares authorized, no shares issued or outstanding, pro forma and pro forma as adjusted | — | — | — |
| Additional paid-in capital | 74,083 | 81,126 | |
| Accumulated deficit | (41,311) | (41,311) | |
| Accumulated other comprehensive income | 1,755 | 1,755 | |
| **Total shareholders’ equity** | $34,533 | $41,582 | |
| **Total capitalization** | $70,693 | $70,693 | $ |

---

**Footnotes to the Capitalization Table** (as provided in the filing):  
1. As of April 30, 2026, no borrowings outstanding under the SpaceX Credit Facility. In May 2026, the facility was amended to increase borrowing capacity up to $5,000 million (Amended SpaceX Credit Facility), maturing May 19, 2031.  
2. As of April 30, 2026, $20,000 million outstanding under the SpaceX Bridge Loan, maturing September 2, 2027, subject to extension.  
3. Includes obligations related to certain AI infrastructure assets recorded as failed sale-leaseback transactions.

---

### Dilutive Securities Not in the Capitalization Table
The capitalization table does **not** list shares underlying stock options, restricted stock units (RSUs), or warrants because they are not issued and outstanding common stock. However, the prospectus discloses the following share reserves as of December 31, 2025 (amounts are pre‑offering and pre‑conversion; in thousands of shares, as presented, the exact post‑IPO numbers will depend on the final offering price):

| | **Class A** | **Class B** | **Class C** | **Class D** |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Outstanding stock options | 10 | 468 | 474 | — |
| Outstanding RSUs | 47 | 43 | 62 | — |
| Future grants under share-based compensation | 161 | — | 383 | — |

These numbers are subject to the Class C Reclassification, the Preferred Conversion, the 2026 Stock Split, and the IPO itself. The final post‑offering dilutive share counts will be filled in once the offering price is set. The prospectus also notes shares reserved for conversion of Class B to Class A (1‑for‑1), and additional reservation under the A&R 2024 Equity Incentive Plan and A&R 2017 Employee Stock Purchase Plan adopted in connection with the offering.

Yes, the prospectus clearly identifies several categories of shares that could be issued after the IPO and would increase the fully diluted share count:

1. **Earnout shares — Elon Musk’s performance stock award**  
   - Assumed in the xAI Merger, the award entitles Mr. Musk to receive 0.20% of the fully diluted capitalization for each of 12 valuation milestones ($1.065T to $6.565T).  
   - One milestone was already achieved pre‑IPO (25,172,695 shares issued).  
   - The remaining 11 milestones, if achieved, will require issuance of additional Class A common stock after the offering. (See preamble section: *“In connection with the xAI Merger, we also assumed a performance stock award… Mr. Musk’s right to receive shares… upon achievement of each of 12 valuation milestones”*).

2. **Contingent merger consideration — Cursor acquisition option**  
   - SpaceX holds an option to acquire Cursor post‑IPO.  
   - If exercised, the consideration would consist of Class A common stock valued at $60.0 billion, with the number of shares determined by the VWAP over the seven trading days before closing (or, if closed before the offering, the last quarterly valuation).  
   - This represents a significant potential share issuance after the IPO. (See chunk: *“The information in this prospectus also does not reflect… the issuance of shares of our Class A common stock if… our board determines to exercise our option to acquire Cursor”*).

3. **Merger consideration shares — EchoStar spectrum transaction**  
   - The purchase of spectrum licenses from EchoStar will be paid partly in Class A common stock (and cash).  
   - The closing is subject to conditions and will occur after the offering, increasing the share count. (See chunk: *“the payment of … shares of Class A common stock and cash consideration which would occur upon closing of our agreement with EchoStar”*).

4. **Over‑allotment option and equity plans**  
   - The underwriters’ 30‑day option to purchase additional shares (standard in an IPO) would increase shares if exercised.  
   - The Equity Plans (2015 Plan, A&R 2024 Plan, etc.) reserve shares for future option/RSU grants, which would further dilute existing holders when issued. (See preamble sections defining Equity Plans and listing outstanding awards.)

The dilution calculations in the prospectus **do not give effect** to these pending or contingent issuances — the pro forma share count and net tangible book value per share only reflect the Class C reclassification, preferred stock conversion, and the IPO itself. However, all of the above items would affect the fully diluted share count once realized.

The draft leaves the base offering and the additional shares blanks, so 66,666,670 likely represents the aggregate amount being registered for sale in the IPO.)

results.content:
The prospectus makes clear that the number of Class A shares to be offered and the initial public offering price range are not yet determined in this filing. They are presented as blanks (placeholders) and will be supplied by a later amendment before the registration statement becomes effective.

**Key indications from the preamble:**

- **“SUBJECT TO COMPLETION” legend:** The cover page states **“SUBJECT TO COMPLETION, DATED … , 2026”** and includes blank-lined spaces for the number of shares (e.g., “We are offering       shares of our Class A common stock”) and the price range (“We expect the initial public offering price to be between $      and $      per share”). This signals the data is provisional.

- **Delaying amendment language:** The preamble notes the registration statement will become effective only upon a further amendment, as required by Section 8(a) of the Securities Act. This is the standard mechanism by which the blank numbers are finalized and inserted.

- **Price determination process described elsewhere:** While the preamble itself does not explain how the figures are set, the underwriting section clarifies that **the initial public offering price will be determined by negotiations between the company and the representatives**, based on market conditions, the company’s prospects, financial performance, and comparable valuations. The number of shares is typically determined jointly in that process and included in an amended filing.

Thus, the number of shares and price range are **placeholders** that will be **completed by amendment** when the final terms are set, with the price specifically resulting from negotiations with the underwriters.

arguments.prompt: What is the exact number of Class A shares to be offered in the IPO as stated on the cover page and throughout the prospectus? Show all instances where the number of shares offered is mentioned in {{spacex_s1}}
What does the filing say about the over-allotment option, including the exact number of additional shares the underwriters can purchase? Show the exact language in {{spacex_s1}}

results.content:
The prospectus is a preliminary draft (subject to completion) and **does not disclose an exact number of Class A shares to be offered**. Every field that would state the quantity is left blank. Below are all instances in the provided material where the number of shares offered is mentioned—each shown with its blank placeholder.

---

### 1. Cover page summary table (first chunk)
> Class A common stock offered by us ..................... | **shares** (or **shares** if the underwriters exercise their option to purchase additional shares of Class A common stock in full).

### 2. Preamble — core offering details
> Class A common stock offered by us .....................
> **shares** (or **shares** if the underwriters exercise their option to purchase additional shares of Class A common stock in full).

### 3. Preamble — initial statements of the offering
> We are offering **shares** of our Class A common stock.

### 4. Capitalization assumptions — shares sold in the offering
> … on **shares** of Class A common stock … after giving effect to … the sale of **shares** of Class A common stock in this offering …

### 5. Dilution table — new investors’ shares purchased
> | New investors in this offering ............ | **Number** (blank) | **Percent** (blank) |

### 6. Underwriting section — over‑allotment option (related, but states the option size)
> We have granted to the underwriters an option … to purchase up to **additional shares** of Class A common stock …

### 7. Use of proceeds — sensitivity analysis (references the number on the cover)
> … assuming no change to the number of shares of our Class A common stock offered by us, **as set forth on the cover page** of this prospectus.

---

**Conclusion:** In every location where the exact number of Class A shares offered is supposed to appear—the cover page table, the offering description, the capitalization assumptions, the dilution table—the figure is left blank. No numerical value is provided anywhere in the prospectus text you shared.

The filing’s over-allotment option is described using placeholder blanks where the number of additional shares would be inserted. The exact language from the underwriting section is:

> “We have granted to the underwriters an option, exercisable for 30 days after the date of this prospectus, to purchase  
> up to **                ** additional shares of Class A common stock at the public offering price listed on the cover page of  
> this prospectus, less underwriting discounts and commissions.”

The same blank appears in the pricing and proceeds table description:

> “The following table shows the per share and total public offering price, underwriting discounts and commissions,  
> and proceeds before expenses to us. These amounts are shown assuming both no exercise and full exercise of the  
> underwriters’ option to purchase up to an additional **                ** shares of Class A common stock.”

Thus, the exact number of additional shares the underwriters can purchase is not provided in the text; it is left as an empty blank.

The key findings:

1. **All three items require amendment disclosure** for precise figures. The cover page, prospectus summary, capitalization table, and beneficial ownership table all leave post-IPO figures blank.

2. However, there are important nuances:

For (i) Post-IPO fully diluted share count:
- The number of shares offered is blank everywhere (cover page, summary, capitalization table)
- The over-allotment size is blank
- The IPO price is blank (needed for treasury stock method)
- The fee table shows 66,666,670 shares registered, but this appears across multiple price tiers ($5.5T-$7.5T aggregate), suggesting it's a placeholder for fee calculation purposes, not the final offering size
- Even if the fee table number were used, fully diluted requires IPO price for options/RSUs, and contingent shares (earnout, Cursor, EchoStar) depend on post-IPO valuations
- **Requires amendment disclosure**

For (ii) Class A versus Class B economic ownership:
- Pre-IPO pro forma split is known: Class A 6,824,581,339 (≈54.5%), Class B 5,695,729,430 (≈45.5%)
- Post-IPO, Class A increases by unknown number of shares; Class B unchanged
- Post-IPO split cannot be determined without final offering size
- **Requires amendment disclosure**

For (iii) Musk's post-IPO voting control:
- Pre-IPO: 85.1% combined voting power, with 93.6% of Class B
- Post-IPO: All percentages are blank
- However, the filing affirmatively states Musk "will hold more than 50% of the combined voting power" and the company will be a "controlled company"
- The exact percentage is not disclosed
- **The qualitative fact of majority control CAN be determined; the exact percentage requires amendment**

arguments.final_result:
## Analysis: What Can Be Determined vs. What Requires Amendment

The SpaceX S-1 filed May 20, 2026 is a preliminary "Subject to Completion" filing. The cover page, prospectus summary, capitalization table, and beneficial ownership table all leave the number of Class A shares offered, the IPO price range, and all post-IPO ownership and voting figures as redacted blanks. The filing states it will become effective only upon a further amendment under Section 8(a) of the Securities Act. Below is the analysis for each item:

---

### (i) Post-IPO Fully Diluted Share Count — **Requires Amendment Disclosure**

**Cannot be determined from the filing as filed.** Three interdependent blanks prevent calculation:

1. **Number of shares offered is blank.** Every instance — the cover page, prospectus summary, capitalization table, dilution table, and underwriting section — uses the placeholder "      shares." The fee table references 66,666,670 shares across escalating aggregate price tiers ($5.5 trillion to $7.5 trillion), but this appears to serve as a fee-calculation placeholder under Rule 457(o) rather than a finalized offering size; the cover page blanks confirm the number is not yet set.

2. **Over-allotment (greenshoe) size is blank.** The underwriting section reads: "up to ____ additional shares."

3. **IPO price is blank.** Fully diluted share count requires the IPO price for the treasury stock method when counting in-the-money options and RSUs. The filing discloses pre-split dilutive securities (options, RSUs, future grant reserves) but post-split, post-IPO dilutive counts depend on the offering price.

Additionally, material contingent share issuances — Musk's 11 remaining earnout milestones (each 0.20% of fully diluted capitalization), the Cursor acquisition option ($60 billion in Class A stock), and the EchoStar spectrum consideration shares — all depend on post-IPO valuations and closing conditions not determinable from the preliminary filing.

---

### (ii) Class A Versus Class B Economic Ownership — **Requires Amendment Disclosure**

**Cannot be precisely determined from the filing as filed.**

The pre-IPO pro forma share counts (after Class C reclassification, preferred conversion, 5-for-1 stock split, and xAI/X Holdings mergers) are disclosed:

| Class | Pro Forma Shares Outstanding | Approximate Economic Ownership |
|-------|------------------------------|-------------------------------|
| Class A | 6,824,581,339 | ~54.5% |
| Class B | 5,695,729,430 | ~45.5% |
| **Total** | **12,520,310,769** | **100%** |

Class A and Class B shares have identical economic rights (pro rata dividends, ratable liquidation). However, post-IPO, Class A outstanding will increase by the unknown number of shares sold to the public while Class B remains unchanged. The capitalization table's "Pro Forma As Adjusted" column leaves Class A shares outstanding blank. Without the final offering size, the post-IPO economic ownership split between Class A and Class B cannot be computed.

---

### (iii) Musk's Post-IPO Voting Control — **Partially Determinable**

**Qualitative conclusion CAN be determined; exact percentage requires amendment.**

The filing explicitly and repeatedly states that immediately after the offering, Elon Musk will hold **more than 50% of the combined voting power** of outstanding common stock. This is disclosed as a definitive fact, not as forward-looking or conditional language. The filing further states SpaceX will qualify as a Nasdaq "controlled company," exempting it from certain board independence requirements. Key supporting facts disclosed:

- Musk beneficially owns **93.6%** of Class B shares (10 votes per share) pre-offering
- Class B holders, voting separately, elect 51% of the board
- Musk can only be removed as CEO/Chairman by a majority vote of Class B shares (which he controls)

However, the **exact post-IPO voting percentage is redacted** — the prospectus uses blanks such as "___%" in every post-offering column of the beneficial ownership table and in the prospectus summary. Pre-offering, Musk's combined voting power is disclosed as **85.1%**.

---

### Summary Table

| Item | Determinable from Filing as Filed? | Requires Amendment? |
|------|-------------------------------------|---------------------|
| (i) Post-IPO fully diluted share count | **No** | **Yes** — offering size, greenshoe, and IPO price all blank |
| (ii) Class A vs. Class B economic ownership | **No** (pre-IPO split is known: ~54.5%/45.5%, but post-IPO depends on blank offering size) | **Yes** |
| (iii) Musk's post-IPO voting control | **Partially** — majority voting control (>50%) is affirmatively disclosed; exact percentage is **not** determinable | **Yes** (for the exact percentage) |

---

### Sources

```json
  "sources": [
    {
      "url": "https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1181412/000162828026036936/spaceexplorationtechnologi.htm",
      "name": "SpaceX S-1 Registration Statement (filed May 20, 2026)"
    }
  ]
}
```
